snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp: Hello, today I saw the protests of Turkish players, and I felt really bad for them. GTA 6 doesn't support their native language, yet they are so excited and haven't lost hope; they are actively using hashtags and messaging Rockstar to get Turkish language support added to the game. Please empathize with the sense of incompleteness that comes from not being able to play a game in your native language, and please be a supporter of them.

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
PewDiePieさん最後まで完成させるなんてすごいです！しかもとてもいい味出しています。
プロの漫画家志望者の90%は完成させられずに挫折するんです。完遂できたことを誇ってください。
素晴らしいです。

趣味で描くだけならこれでいいのですが、実際に本にするなら、原稿用紙の使い方が少し間違っています。
DELITERの原稿用紙は印刷範囲のメモリが青い線で印刷されてるはずです。
用紙いっぱいに描いても、実際に印刷される範囲内しか印刷されません。それ以上書くと印刷時に裁断されて見切れてしまいます。
本を開いた時、見開きいっぱいに描くやり方ですが、2枚の原稿用紙を使って描くのです。

日本に住んでいるなら、是非コミケに自費出版してみてはどうでしょうか？
同人誌出版ならとても敷居は低く、日本国内の印刷業者は高品質で他国よりリーズナブルで印刷できます。
コミックマーケット108は8月15〜16日に開催されます。108回目のコミケはもう出展応募は締め切っています。
アマチュア作家やプロの作家達が自費出版して会場で販売するイベントです。一度体験してみてください。
108回目のコミケに行って、次の109回目のコミケの参加申し込み用紙を入手してください。
109回目のコミケは12月29日〜12月31日に開催されます。
きっとあなたの作品を読みたいと思って来場するファンは沢山いるでしょう。

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
The more times you draw, even if it is the same image, the better you get. However, sometimes you need to move to a different image for a while and then go back to the first one.
Sometimes switching it up and taking a break helps your mind from getting overloaded and stressing out.
Just like writer's block, you need to write something or draw something different to keep your creative thoughts going. You get stuck on one thing longer than you think you need to, and then the rest of your work suffers from it. Been there, done that, and still facing it too.

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
This is really cool, it's quite inspiring <3
I've been drawing like my whole life, sometimes I kinda lose the motivation though and don't draw much, but this and all the other drawing vids makes me want to draw something immediately.
The skill level growth is amazing too. As I said, I've been drawing many years, so I see some things here where I see an error or something like "I could probably do that a bit better", but then there's other stuff it's like "wow, that's better than what I could do" and he's got there in such a short amount of time. Everyone has their own strengths and weaknesses, it's really cool to see

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp: People love shitting on him waah waah millionaire waah waah he has time. Bruv watching him genuenly enjoy the process, of whatever he dicided to dabble in gave me more hope and creative boost than listening to whining of supposedly full time creatives. He's tackling and solving problems, looking for ways to overcome whatever issue he encounters, it's fun, it's engaging, it makes me want to do things man. snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp: This feels like me when I decided that I would read an entire book in traditional Chinese; starting out I vastly overestimated my vocabulary and everyone said not to start reading a regular book but to work yourself up by starting with simple children-level books and gradually moving on towards more advanced stuff, but no I am stubborn so I happily/angrily spend 30 minutes per page cross reading with a dictionary and still going. I'll be several years older when I finally get through the whole thing but dammit I started it, I learn heaps along the way, and I'll get the chance to be proud of myself when it's finally finished, so I won't listen to the people telling me I'm in over my head :) Thanks for the inspo Felix!
snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textOrig: This feels like me when I decided that I would read an entire book in traditional Chinese; starting out I vastly overestimated my vocabulary and everyone said not to start reading a regular book but to work yourself up by starting with simple children-level books and gradually moving on towards more advanced stuff, but no I am stubborn so I happily/angrily spend 30 minutes per page cross reading with a dictionary and still going. I'll be several years older when I finally get through the whole thing but dammit I started it, I learn heaps along the way, and I'll get the chance to be proud of myself when it's finally finished, so I won't listen to the people telling me I'm in over my head :) Thanks for the inspo Felix!

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
"It's so hard. I have a lot of breakdowns. I'll never be that good." It does feel that way when you start big art projects like a manga. Some pro artists have more fun drawing smaller projects first, like silly fun comics. And then work up to bigger projects. It's impressive that pewds can pull through, but there are healthier ways to achieve drawing your manga without having to torture yourself, and avoid burnout. Focus on drawing a part of your manga everyday, and the rest of the Manga will reveal itself over time. Also I don't think I noticed any manga tutorial books in your video? The manga artists are really good inspiration, but there are some good ones out there that can help you understand the fundementals.

Edit: I probably commented to early before finishing the video.

Focus on drawing a part of your manga everyday, and the rest of the Manga will reveal itself over time.  Also I don't think I noticed any manga tutorial books in your video? snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
On the note of finishing pieces, one of my friends always said it's like XP in a video game. You get way more experience for finishing the level (completing an artwork) than you do for defeating a few enemies (starting a bunch of pieces and never finishing them). It's a dumb analogy, but it really stuck with me.

Honestly, I think it's true. You improve way faster by finishing a bunch of smaller pieces than by having a folder full of giant unfinished projects. The act of pushing a piece all the way to the end is where a lot of the learning happens and it clearly shows in how quickly you have grown your art!!

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp: Hey man, you might not read this, but here it goes anyways. I'm 38 (almost 39) years old guy, that has a wife an a 8 years old son. So I think we're pretty much from the same generation. I've followed you since 2012 more or less. I somewhat felt like I envied you, because you can do all this stuff that I always wanted to do but always had to put down or postpone indefinitely because I didn't have the time or whatever reason - however this video hit something different, and I noticed you and I have something in common, throughout the video you like criticized yourself or I noticed that you had your doubts or concerns, but you kept forward, you kept at it, you had lows and ups, but you kept pressing forward, and you finished it. You finished your own manga, and that's no small feat, considering you have a wife and a son, and I guess a lot of other things to do, but you kept pushing for something you wanted to do, and I felt really happy for you, by the end, when you read your finished product. And I could sense all the love and effort you put on, and that got me thinking. There's always a phrase my wife tells me... "Better done than perfect".. You don't need it to be perfect, you need it to be done. So, congratulations man. You did really well, and you should be proud of what you made. I will try to do as well.

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
I wanna remind anyone who sees this: even the most famous manga have some janky ass frames, sometimes characters be looking wild from one close up to another, and other times it looks better. But the mangaka still published, and the story gets loved. You ever read the first volume of attack on titan? Some of those shots are insanely goofy.

All this to say it's good to put out good work, but don't let yourself get stuck on the details and perfecting every frame, sometimes it's better to just get it done and get your story out there

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
When I first read BLAME! I would listen to a playlist of orchestal music and every so often the playlist would end during an intense scene and I would be so immersed I couldnt stop reading to restart it.

When the video went silent it captured those feelings all over again. The lack of dialogue, the heavy emotions and the silence.

Felix I think you've done a great job of replicating the feeling of BLAME! and you should be proud. 😊

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
Damn my mental space isn't too great today so that ending got to me a bit. However, despite that, this is a beautiful piece of art. There are many ways to interpret this piece, and many ways people can find ways to relate in their own lives. I don't think the story matters all that much, it's the feelings you get living through the little boy, the tiger, the hunter, and the moon.

Bravo Pewds. An artists work is never "finished", never "perfect", but you got a A+ in the feels department for sure. And that's what true art does, it captures feelings.

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
Hey bro. When you said "I have to draw for at least six years to be a master" that kinda
resonated with me. I've been drawing for at least 14 years and studied art for 4 years. And still I have that thought of "this much time till I'll be a master" and I kinda learned that this thought always stays with you. The progress that one imagines is a lot faster than reality. The thought of what you should be or could be like in the next years can negatively affect on your perspective with how far you have come. For you to take on the challenge to make a manga is a big deal and you can be very proud of what you created. You are not a master compared to someone else maybe. But you are the best that you can do right now. Basically speaking. You have mastered yourself.

The thought of what you should be or could be like in the next years can negatively affect on your perspective with how far you have come. For you to take on the challenge to make a manga is a big deal and you can be very proud of what you created. You are not a master compared to someone else maybe. But you are the best that you can do right now. Basically speaking. You have mastered yourself.

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
Wow… that unexpectedly hit me hard
I feel like the idea is so individually obvious to anyone, everyone sees the exact thing they got touched by one day, it’s so worldwide and uniting to me
This is truly a unique piece
And i get that such massive youtuber has to chuckle at their own work not to get harsh critique but the drawing is sooooo good
The use of all the tools and the tones is so beautiful, it makes it so whole
I feel like that could be too personal for me to share such deep and intimate peice as such story
I just want to thank you for the bravery and the true spirit of art!!
Stay safe and creative, everyone!!

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
As someone also working on their first graphic novel (comics for me, not manga) its really comforting to see someone else also go through a lot of the same issues I did. I wasn't nearly as ambitious as pewds-- I foresaw the amount of work and deiced to make a short 8-10 page comic. Still, I had that same moment of realization where you think "Wow this is WAY more work than I expected!"
Still, got to keep sticking it out until its complete!

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
Hey Pewds, I was just thinking as I watched through your final manga that the most impressive thing from this is that you finished it, just like that artist said to you. Creative work really is about finishing the thing and moving on, because each time you do, you grow in a way that it is hard to perceive right away, but that is always the key: To finish and move on.

I work in animation with storyboard and writting and here are my technical thoughts on it if it interests you: Your drawings are super good and love your wide shots. You could make use of the rule of thirds/less centered focus for a next one as that could bring a bit of variety and push the feeling you want to express, especially when you do something purely visual (no dialogue) as this. In terms of writing and the story, your instinct to cut things is absolutely right - the best stories are told with a lot of subtext and implications rather than showing everything, except for specific moments that deserve extra attention. The ending felt like it needed that, but some of the first few shots could've been cut like you said.

It's up to you if you want to take or leave my feedback, but the last thing I'd say from creator to creator is: always listen to your gut! Sometimes you can't explain it, but that is part of the magic in art, a lot of it is made by feeling what you should or should not do. Again, impressive work overall and especially that you push through it all and finished it. I hope you take time to rest, but then continue with your next artistic adventure because you obviously feel joy from it and have an artistic spirit

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp: 3:19 I usually draw out the manga page layout in the storyboarding faze, that way, if I don’t like how the flow is then it’s easier to erase and redo. It basically ends up looking like a bunch of tiny manga pages next to each other. It helps a lot with seeing how each page will flow with each other as well. (I wish I could include a picture but I hope my explanation is good enough) I find that this method helps me more, also looking at different manga and how they have paneled their pages help in order to make your paneling more interesting

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
Hey Felix, I just wanted to say a huge thank you. I’ve been watching you for over 14 years, and I’m 28 now — it’s crazy to think how much you’ve influenced my life.
Your recommendations got me into anime, and I even read ‘Blame!’ all the way through — I bought every printed volume back when the translation first came out im my country. It’s cool to see that you drew manga inspired by our somewhat similar experiences of reading it :) It looking daaaaamn good tho. I'm loving it!
Because of you, I also started climbing. That brought me a lot of new friends and healthiness into my life. And honestly, your content make me want that same kind of happiness someday - loving family and a kid. Haha. Thank you so much, Felix. I’m really glad I got to grow up with you. You’ve genuinely inspired me in so many ways.

Because of you, I also started climbing. That brought me a lot of new friends and healthiness into my life. And honestly, your content make me want that same kind of happiness someday - loving family and a kid. Haha. Thank you so much, Felix. I’m really glad I got to grow up with you. snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp:
I’m reading the end as „He‘s the big one now.“ Whatever this shall mean - I actually think it‘s a great idea to let the reader make up his own story 🙏🏼
And thanks for also sharing your struggles along the way, I almost hate that I can relate so much 😂
The „I‘m aware of it and I feel it, so I hope I can break through“ is probably what I‘m taking with me from this video - well, at least giving it my best to do so.

I don’t have to throw away work that’s 90% there - it‘s probably an illusion that it‘s complete bull****. when „I‘m aware and I feel it; I can find a way to handle it.“

Thank you! 😊🙏🏼

молодец конечно, адский труд. большинство бы просто сдались
но "писать сценарий было легче всего" очевидно бред. Просто так кажется из-за того что человек 0 в этом, что видно по истории
Поэтому тот профи сказал, чтобы не грубить "ты закончил и это хорошо". потому что в истории много нелогичного и странного что тяжело представить даже в вымышленном мире, зачем запрыгивать на тигра, который тебя хочет сожрать, когда перед тобой охотник, зачем тигру в воду прыгать чтобы скинуть, если у него миллион других способов сбросить его и тд тп

snippet.topLevelComment.snippet.textDisp: Unfortunately some of you don't seem to understand that there really is no case here that can be taken to trial. Flight logs, emails and guest list are not pieces of evidence that could sustain a trail, of which a guilty verdict can be found. You need digital footprints, rape kits or multiple witnesses with abilities to prove their whereabouts. When a crime is committed you need to report it as soon as it happens, because there is a window of time to acquire evidence that can be used to get a guilty verdict. Don't wait until the situation becomes public and then come out and make accusations of wrongdoings because you are trying to get involved in a possible classaction lawsuit. There is nothing that they have that could sustain a trial, you people have to understand this, I'm not defending these people but I am seeing the same responses related to this case. When you get a speeding ticket, the court recognizes the radar gun as a tool which is operated by a person designated as a officer or the law. The ability to have a guilty verdict is because of a radar gun being operated by law enforcement. This gun is under strict policy to be calibrated and documented as an instrument. You are suggesting that a cop walk into court and tell a judge he just visually saw him speeding and "trust me bro" when you say you want this case to go to trial, that is not how the court system works.

Flight logs, emails and guest list are not pieces of evidence that could sustain a trail, of which a guilty verdict can be found. You need digital footprints, rape kits or multiple witnesses with abilities to prove their whereabouts. When a crime is committed you need to report it as soon as it happens, because there is a window of time to acquire evidence that can be used to get a guilty verdict. Don't wait until the situation becomes public and then come out and make accusations of wrongdoings because you are trying to get involved in a possible classaction lawsuit. There is nothing that they have that could sustain a trial, you people have to understand this, I'm not defending these people but I am seeing the same responses related to this case. When you get a speeding ticket, the court recognizes the radar gun as a tool which is operated by a person designated as a officer or the law. The ability to have a guilty verdict is because of a radar gun being operated by law enforcement. This gun is under strict policy to be calibrated and documented as an instrument. You are suggesting that a cop walk into court and tell a judge he just visually saw him speeding and "trust me bro" when you say you want this case to go to trial, that is not how the court system works.
